frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: perceptual inference treated as combining sensory likelihood with prior beliefs

frameworks.name: feedback-based perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: feedback reliability shapes the weighting of sensory evidence versus priors

core_question: How does corrupted (unreliable) feedback during a perceptual task alter the balance between sensory evidence and prior beliefs in subsequent inference?
key_assumptions: observer combines sensory likelihood with priors via Bayes rule
feedback reliability modulates trust in sensory information
predictive auditory cues can induce task-relevant priors
manipulations: feedback type during intervention runs (corrupted vs uncorrupted)
presence of predictive auditory cues inducing priors in tests
key_findings: corrupted feedback reduced perceptual performance whereas uncorrupted did not
reliance on prior beliefs increased after corrupted feedback
simulation reproduced the empirical decrease in performance and prior reliance shift
exploratory analyses showed reduced perceptual confidence during corrupted feedback
interpretations: unreliable feedback compromises sensory processing and biases inference toward priors
feedback reliability has sustained downstream effects on the prior-likelihood balance
limitations_noted: confidence results based on exploratory analyses
